name: Brian Houston
role: Founder and Global Senior Pastor, Hillsong Church
incident: Brian Houston, founder of Hillsong Church, resigned on March 21, 2022 after an independent investigation found he had behaved inappropriately toward two women at separate church conferences over a decade. Houston had built Hillsong into a global brand on messages of moral integrity and family values. The same investigation also revealed Houston had failed to report his father's child sexual abuse to authorities.

name: Creflo Dollar
role: Senior Pastor, World Changers Church International, College Park, Georgia
incident: Creflo Dollar, prosperity gospel televangelist and founder of World Changers Church International, launched a fundraising campaign in March 2015 asking his congregation and online followers to donate $65 million toward a Gulfstream G650 private jet. Dollar argued the jet was needed for global ministry. Dollar was already operating two Gulfstream jets at the time of the fundraising request and was simultaneously teaching that financial giving to his ministry was the path to personal financial blessing.

name: Mark Driscoll
role: Founder, Mars Hill Church, Seattle, Washington; Founder, Trinity Church, Scottsdale, Arizona
incident: Mark Driscoll, who built Mars Hill Church in Seattle to 15,000 members while preaching male authority, spiritual submission, and accountability, was forced to resign from Mars Hill in 2014 after investigations documented years of bullying, plagiarism, and misuse of church funds. Driscoll subsequently founded Trinity Church in Scottsdale, where former members and staff documented the same pattern of behavior beginning in 2021, including spiritual abuse, financial misconduct, and firing staff who raised concerns.
